steps.title: Combine the shared factor
steps.content:
Both terms contain $x+7$, so factor it out.
$$(x+7)(\frac{1}{3}-\frac{1}{2})=-8$$
Since
$$\frac{1}{3}-\frac{1}{2}=-\frac{1}{6}$$
the equation becomes
$$-\frac{1}{6}(x+7)=-8$$

steps.title: Isolate the variable
steps.content:
Multiply both sides by $-6$, then subtract $7$.
$$x+7=48$$
$$x=41$$

steps.title: Calculator shortcut
steps.content: Entering the original equation in a graphing calculator or Desmos gives the same solution for $x$. The answer is C.
